content.text: LangSmith is LangChain's observability and evaluation platform for LLM applications. You do not need to use LangChain to use LangSmith — it works with any Python code via the @traceable decorator or the RunTree API. The core data model has three levels: a Project groups all runs for one application or environment, a Run is a single unit of execution (a chain, LLM call, tool call, or retrieval), and Feedback is a key/score/comment object attached to any run. Every run knows its parent_run_id, giving you a nested tree for a complete agent turn.

attrs.alt: LangSmith data model showing Projects, Runs nested by parent, and Feedback

content.text: Enable tracing by setting four environment variables. Once they are set, any code decorated with @traceable or wrapped in trace() sends runs to the LangSmith project automatically — no explicit client calls needed in most cases.

pip install langsmith anthropic

export LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2="true"
export LANGCHAIN_API_KEY="ls__..."
export LANGCHAIN_PROJECT="support-agent-prod"   # project name
export LANGCHAIN_ENDPOINT="https://api.smith.langchain.com"  # default

The @traceable decorator converts any Python function into a traced run. LangSmith infers the run_type from the arguments you pass to the decorator. Nested @traceable functions automatically become child runs under the closest ancestor, forming the tree without any explicit parent-id wiring.

When you need full control — logging extra inputs, outputs, or metadata that the decorator cannot infer — use RunTree directly. Each RunTree object represents one run; you call .post() to create it, then .patch() to update with outputs and end time.

from langsmith.run_trees import RunTree
import anthropic, os

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
PROJECT = os.getenv("LANGCHAIN_PROJECT", "default")


def run_agent_manual(user_message: str, user_id: str) -> str:
    # Root run
    root = RunTree(
        name="agent_turn",
        run_type="chain",
        project_name=PROJECT,
        inputs={"message": user_message},
        tags=["production", f"user:{user_id}"],
        extra={"metadata": {"agent_version": "2.4.1"}},
    )
    root.post()  # creates the run in LangSmith

    # Child LLM run
    llm_run = root.create_child(
        name="llm_call",
        run_type="llm",
        inputs={"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_message}]},
    )
    llm_run.post()

    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}],
    )
    answer = resp.content[0].text

    llm_run.end(
        outputs={"text": answer},
        extra={
            "invocation_params": {"model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"},
            "usage": {
                "prompt_tokens": resp.usage.input_tokens,
                "completion_tokens": resp.usage.output_tokens,
            },
        },
    )
    llm_run.patch()  # send final state

    root.end(outputs={"answer": answer})
    root.patch()
    return answer

Feedback in LangSmith maps to a (key, score) pair linked to a run_id. Scores are floats; you can also store a string comment. The most common patterns are inline user ratings collected from your UI and offline LLM-judge scores computed in a background pipeline.

from langsmith import Client

ls_client = Client()


def record_thumbs(run_id: str, thumbs_up: bool) -> None:
    ls_client.create_feedback(
        run_id=run_id,
        key="user_feedback",
        score=1.0 if thumbs_up else 0.0,
        comment="from thumbs widget",
    )


def score_faithfulness(run_id: str, answer: str, context: str) -> None:
    judge_prompt = (
        f"Context: {context}\n\nAnswer: {answer}\n\n"
        "Rate faithfulness 0–1. Reply with only a number."
    )
    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=10,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": judge_prompt}],
    )
    try:
        score = float(resp.content[0].text.strip())
    except ValueError:
        return
    ls_client.create_feedback(
        run_id=run_id,
        key="faithfulness",
        score=score,
    )


# Categorical feedback via score + comment
def label_run(run_id: str, category: str) -> None:
    ls_client.create_feedback(
        run_id=run_id,
        key="response_category",
        score=1.0,
        comment=category,  # e.g. "factual", "refusal", "hallucination"
    )

A LangSmith Dataset is a named collection of Example objects, each holding inputs and optionally reference outputs. You run your agent against each example with run_on_dataset(), which creates one run per example under a named experiment, then evaluates each with evaluator functions that return a dict with key and score.

from langsmith import Client
from langsmith.evaluation import evaluate

ls_client = Client()

# Create dataset
ls_client.create_dataset("support-qa", description="Support agent golden set")
for item in [
    {"inputs": {"message": "Reset password?"}, "outputs": {"answer": "Use the reset link..."}},
    {"inputs": {"message": "Order status?"},   "outputs": {"answer": "Check tracking page..."}},
]:
    ls_client.create_example(**item, dataset_name="support-qa")


# Evaluator: check if expected answer substring appears in output
def check_contains(run, example) -> dict:
    expected = example.outputs.get("answer", "").lower()
    actual = (run.outputs or {}).get("answer", "").lower()
    return {"key": "contains", "score": 1.0 if expected[:30] in actual else 0.0}


# Target function — receives Example inputs, returns outputs dict
def agent_target(inputs: dict) -> dict:
    return {"answer": run_agent(inputs["message"])}


# Run evaluation
results = evaluate(
    agent_target,
    data="support-qa",
    evaluators=[check_contains],
    experiment_prefix="haiku-v2",
    max_concurrency=4,
)
print(results.to_pandas()[["feedback.contains"]].mean())

Use list_runs() to pull recent runs for an offline scoring pipeline. The query supports filtering by project, run_type, tags, time range, and error status. Combining list_runs() with create_feedback() gives you an incremental scoring loop that ignores already-scored runs.

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from langsmith import Client

ls_client = Client()


def score_recent_unscored(project: str, key: str = "faithfulness") -> None:
    since = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=1)
    runs = ls_client.list_runs(
        project_name=project,
        run_type="chain",
        start_time=since,
        filter=f'and(eq(status, "success"), not(has(feedback_key, "{key}")))',
    )
    for run in runs:
        answer = (run.outputs or {}).get("answer", "")
        if not answer:
            continue
        score_faithfulness(str(run.id), answer, context="")


# Inspect a specific run
run = ls_client.read_run("<run-uuid>")
print(run.name, run.status, run.total_tokens, run.total_cost)
print([f.key for f in ls_client.list_feedback(run_ids=[str(run.id)])])
The evaluate() function returns an ExperimentResults object. Sum the contains scores to gate CI: exit 1 if the mean falls below your threshold, otherwise exit 0.

import sys, os

MIN_SCORE = 0.80


def ci_gate() -> None:
    results = evaluate(
        agent_target,
        data="support-qa",
        evaluators=[check_contains],
        experiment_prefix=f"ci-{os.getenv('GIT_SHA', 'local')}",
    )
    df = results.to_pandas()
    mean = df["feedback.contains"].mean()
    n = len(df)
    print(f"contains: {mean:.2%} ({n} examples)")
    if mean < MIN_SCORE:
        print(f"FAIL: {mean:.2%} < {MIN_SCORE:.2%}")
        sys.exit(1)
    print("PASS")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    ci_gate()

LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2 not set — without this, @traceable is a no-op: the code runs fine but nothing is sent to LangSmith, and there is no error message. Always verify with a quick smoke-test run and check the LangSmith UI for the project.

Forgetting to call root.patch() with RunTree — post() creates the run in LangSmith but sets its status to "pending". Only patch() closes it with final outputs and end_time. A run stuck in pending appears as an error in the UI.
Using list_runs() without a time filter — without start_time, list_runs iterates the entire project history, which can be millions of runs. Always bound your query by time or run count.
Running evaluate() with high concurrency against a slow agent — max_concurrency defaults to no limit. If your agent calls a rate-limited API, set max_concurrency=2 or 4 to avoid burst errors.

@traceable works with async functions without any change to the decorator. LangSmith propagates context through Python's asyncio task context automatically, so nested async @traceable calls attach to the correct parent run even across await boundaries.

import asyncio
from langsmith import traceable
import anthropic

client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()


@traceable(run_type="chain", name="async_agent_turn")
async def run_agent_async(user_message: str) -> str:
    # Both child calls become child runs under the same trace
    docs = await retrieve_async(user_message)
    return await respond_async(user_message, docs)


@traceable(run_type="retrieval")
async def retrieve_async(query: str) -> list[str]:
    await asyncio.sleep(0.01)  # simulate async I/O
    return [f"doc: {query}"]


@traceable(run_type="llm")
async def respond_async(message: str, docs: list[str]) -> str:
    resp = await client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"{docs}\n{message}"}],
    )
    return resp.content[0].text


async def main():
    result = await run_agent_async("What is the refund policy?")
    print(result)


asyncio.run(main())

Tags are strings you add to a run for categorical filtering in the LangSmith UI (e.g. 'production', 'canary', 'v2.4'). Metadata is a free-form dict that stores richer context — Git SHA, user tier, model config — that you can search against. Both are set either via the decorator or by calling langsmith.get_current_run_tree() to attach them at runtime.

import os
from langsmith import traceable, get_current_run_tree

GIT_SHA = os.getenv("GIT_SHA", "local")
ENV = os.getenv("DEPLOY_ENV", "development")


@traceable(
    run_type="chain",
    name="agent_turn",
    tags=[ENV, "support"],          # static tags on every call
    metadata={"agent_version": "2.4.1", "git_sha": GIT_SHA},
)
def run_agent_tagged(user_message: str, user_tier: str) -> str:
    # Add runtime tags / metadata not known at decoration time
    rt = get_current_run_tree()
    if rt:
        rt.tags = rt.tags or []
        rt.tags.append(f"tier:{user_tier}")
        rt.extra = rt.extra or {}
        rt.extra.setdefault("metadata", {})["user_tier"] = user_tier

    return call_model(user_message)


# In the LangSmith UI filter:
#   tag = "production" AND tag = "tier:pro"
# to isolate pro-tier production traces.

LangSmith traces any Python code via @traceable (zero-boilerplate, nested runs automatic) or RunTree (full control). Feedback attaches scores to any run with create_feedback(). Datasets + evaluate() run your agent against a golden set, compute scores with custom evaluators, and return a Pandas-compatible result object you can gate CI on. Query runs offline with list_runs() to build incremental scoring pipelines. For teams that cannot use a proprietary SaaS, Langfuse with MIT self-hosting is the equivalent alternative.
